title: Ozoroa dispar
intro: Ozoroa dispar, also known as the red stinkwood, is a species of plant in the Burseraceae family, native to tropical regions of Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its potential to reduce inflammation, alleviate pain, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a promising natural remedy for various health issues. The bioactive constituents of Ozoroa dispar include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
